MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO INTERVENE (TRANSACTION ID # 100117582) FILED BY INTERVENOR MONTGOMERY SANSOME, LP
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-12-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - INTERVENOR MONTGOMERY SANSOME, LP'S MOTION TO INTERVENE IS GRANTED. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO IMPOSE CONDITIONS ON THE INTERVENTION AS THE LITIGATION WILL RESOLVE THE ADDUCED ISSUES. INTERVENOR HAS DEMONSTRATED THE RIGHT TO INTERVENE IN THIS ACTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 387(D)(1)(B) ["THE PERSON SEEKING INTERVENTION CLAIMS AN INTEREST RELATING TO THE PROPERTY OR TRANSACTION THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE ACTION AND THAT PERSON IS SO SITUATED THAT THE DISPOSITION OF THE ACTION MAY IMPAIR OR IMPEDE THAT PERSON'S ABILITY TO PROTECT THAT INTEREST, UNLESS THAT PERSON'S INTEREST IS ADEQUATELY REPRESENTED BY ONE OR MORE OF THE EXISTING PARTIES."] INTERVENOR CONTENDS THAT IT IS PLAINTIFF'S ASSIGNEE AND ENTITLED TO RECOVER FOR ITS WORK UNDER DEFENDANTS' INSURANCE POLICIES. INTERVENOR THEREFORE HAS AN INTEREST IN HOW THIS CASE IS LITIGATED. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT INTERVENOR'S ASSIGNMENT IS DISPUTED ACTUALLY DEMONSTRATES WHY INTERVENOR NEEDS TO INTERVENE. IF THERE IS A CONFLICT REGARDING THE VIABILITY OF THE ASSIGNMENT, THEN INTERVENOR NEEDS TO INTERVENE TO ENSURE THAT ITS RIGHTS ARE ADEQUATELY REPRESENTED. (SEE HODGE V. KIRKPATRICK DEVELOP., INC. (2005) 130 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 555 [INTERVENTION PROPER WHERE PARTIES HAVE CONFLICTING INTERESTS].) INTERVENTION IS PROPER TO ACCORD COMPLETE RELIEF AMONG ALL OF THE PARTIES AND INTERVENTION WILL NOT UNDULY BROADEN THE ISSUES TO BE RESOLVED IN THIS CASE. THIS DEPARTMENT WILL NOT MODIFY THE SCHEDULING OF THE CMC MATTERS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Continued To Nov-24-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT MONTGOMERY SANSOME, LP'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1060, Cross-Complainant Kemper Independence Insurance Company properly seeks declaratory relief regarding its insurance policy with Plaintiff Williams. (See Cross-complaint, pars. 1, 28, 32-34 & Prayer par. 1.) In addition, Kemper has standing to seek declaratory relief regarding the alleged assignment of policy benefits from Williams to Montgomery even though it is not a party to the assignment. Kemper has an "interest" in the assignment as the existence and scope of the assignment directly relates to how Kemper will process the claim. (See Gardiner v. Gaither (1958) 162 Cal.App.2d 607, 621-22 [assignee of creditor could seek declaratory relief regarding the existence of a partnership agreement even though the assignee was not a party to the partnership agreement]; Olson v. Toy (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 818, 824-25 ["[t]his section [CCP Sec. 1060] does not require that plaintiffs and defendants be parties to, or that plaintiffs be interested under, a legal instrument as a predicate to plaintiffs maintaining a declaratory relief action."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Continued To Apr-28-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Intervene
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, STEPHEN S. SAYAD (Email: Stephensayad@gmail.com) of the law offices of STEPHEN S. SAYAD (Tel: 415.666.6163) appears as counsel for plaintiff, Clarence Williams

Attorney, Brian D. Harrison (Brian.Harrison@clydeco.us) of the law offices of CLYDE & CO US LLP (Tel: 415.365.9800) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Attorney, Lisa D. Angelo (langelo@murchisonlaw.com) of the law offices of Murchison & Cumming LLP (Tel: 213.630.1023) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Concise Insurance Services

Attorney, Hugo Torbet of the law offices of Hugo Torbet (Tel: 415.986.9400) appears as counsel for intervenor/moving party, Montgomery Sansome, LP

INTERVENOR MONTGOMERY SANSOME, LP Motion And Notice Of Motion To Intervene

The cause is argued and taken under submission as reflected in the record of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Intervenor Montgomery Sansome, LP's motion to intervene is GRANTED. The Court denies defendant's request to impose conditions on the intervention as the litigation will resolve the adduced issues. Intervenor has demonstrated the right to intervene in this action under Code of Civil Procedure section 387(d)(1)(B) ["The person seeking intervention claims an interest relating to the property or transaction that is the subject of the action and that person is so situated that the disposition of the action may impair or impede that person's ability to protect that interest, unless that person's interest is adequately represented by one or more of the existing parties."] Intervenor contends that it is plaintiff's assignee and entitled to recover for its work under defendants' insurance policies. Intervenor therefore has an interest in how this case is litigated. Defendant's argument that intervenor's assignment is disputed actually demonstrates why intervenor needs to intervene. If there is a conflict regarding the viability of the assignment, then intervenor needs to intervene to ensure that its rights are adequately represented. (See Hodge v. Kirkpatrick Develop., Inc. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 540, 555 [intervention proper where parties have conflicting interests].) Intervention is proper to accord complete relief among all of the parties and intervention will not unduly broaden the issues to be resolved in this case. This department will not modify the scheduling of the CMC matters. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.
